project_overview.project_development_objective: To increase access to safely managed water services in Greater Conakry and to strengthen the operational and organizational performance of water and sanitation institutions in Guinea
project_overview.total_financing: US$499.092 million (IDA US$200 million credit, GEF US$10.092 million grant, EIB-EU US$274 million, GoG US$15 million counterpart funding)
project_overview.beneficiaries: 2,612,467 people total (572,200 newly connected to water services, 2,040,267 existing customers with improved services); 3,578 students in 400 secondary schools; sector institution personnel; local residents in Kankoure basin

primary_locations: Greater Conakry (Kaloum, Dixinn, Matam, Ratoma, Gbessia)
geographic_scope.coverage_type: Regional (Greater Conakry focus) with water resources management in selected upstream areas
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: Partial - Guinea has a transitional government (CNRD) following a coup in September 2021; political and governance risk rated HIGH; no active armed conflict but political instability is a concern

key_components.component_name: Component 1: Increasing and improving water services in Greater Conakry
key_components.description: Construction of Yessoulou IV water treatment plant (200,000 m3/day capacity with 1MW solar PV system); storage tanks and distribution networks (117.5 km pipelines, 9,370 household connections, 24 standposts); non-revenue water reduction through performance-based contracts; compensation for project-affected persons
key_components.financing: US$435 million (IDA US$165 million, EIB-EU US$255 million, GoG US$15 million)

key_components.component_name: Component 2: Support to Water Sector Performance and Institutional and Policy Reforms
key_components.description: Urban water sector reforms including tariff policy implementation; SEG utility strengthening through NEMA program; sanitation governance reforms and creation of national sanitation company; WASH facilities in 100 secondary schools; support for fecal sludge service providers professionalization

key_components.component_name: Component 3: Enhancing Water Resources Management in Selected Areas
key_components.description: Nature-based solutions for soil erosion control and reservoir sedimentation in Samou sub-basin; community engagement through Basin Committee establishment; protection perimeters for water supply boreholes; support for Gangan National Park management
key_components.financing: US$11.262 million (GEF US$9.262 million, IDA US$2 million)

key_components.description: Project coordination unit staffing and operations; project management firm (AMO) support; technical assistance to implementing agencies; monitoring and evaluation; independent verification of performance-based conditions
key_components.financing: US$28.830 million (EIB-EU US$19 million, IDA US$9 million, GEF US$830,000)

key_components.description: Zero-funded component allowing rapid reallocation of funds for emergency response and disaster recovery

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Energy, Water Resources and Hydrocarbons (MEHH) with Project Coordination Unit (PCU); Société des Eaux de Guinée (SEG) as technical partner for water infrastructure; National Directorate of Land Use Planning and Urban Development (DATU) for sanitation; Guinean Office of National Parks and Wildlife Reserves (OGPNRF) for nature-based solutions; National Directorate of Hydraulics (DNH) for water resources management
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Project Steering Committee chaired by MEHH Minister; collaboration agreements between PCU and implementing agencies (SEG, DATU, OGPNRF, DNH); joint implementation with EIB for coordinated civil works; regular portfolio reviews with World Bank and Ministry of Economy and Finance
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank's New Procurement Framework (NPF) for Investment Project Financing; use of STEP online procurement planning tool; Hands-on Expanded Implementation Support (HEIS) for procurement capacity building; prior and post review arrangements; project management firm (AMO) to support procurement and contract management
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Quarterly and annual progress reports; GEMS (Geo-Enabling Initiative for Monitoring and Supervision) for activity mapping; corporate scorecard methodology for key indicators; gender-disaggregated data collection; mid-term and end-of-project evaluations; independent verification agency for performance-based conditions; annual financial audits by external auditor

identified_risks.risk_description: Guinea has a transitional government (CNRD) following September 2021 coup; unclear timeline for elections and return to civilian rule; risk of policy changes during project implementation
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Strengthened policy dialogue under World Bank Country Management Unit leadership; pipeline DPF with prior actions for water sector reforms; careful selection of sector reforms based on strong analytics; regular dialogue with authorities to address departures from sector strategies

identified_risks.risk_description: Incomplete information on Simandou iron operations revenue (launching 2026); challenges in revenue mobilization from mining companies; difficulties managing expenditures and domestic debt; exposure to commodity market volatility
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Government adoption of measures to mitigate risks; engagement with IMF; support from other development partners; tariff reforms to improve water sector financial viability

identified_risks.risk_description: Risk of policy changes in water and sanitation sector due to transitional government; potential departure from current sector strategies
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Selection of sector reforms based on strong analytics from previous studies; funding and institutional measures to strengthen regulator AREE; constant dialogue with authorities; institutional roadmap for key institutions

identified_risks.risk_description: Standard water infrastructure investments; feasibility studies and bidding documents already developed; institutional strengthening activities require technical expertise
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Use of standard water investment designs; feasibility studies and bidding documents developed and reviewed by World Bank; technical assistance firms for institutional strengthening activities

identified_risks.risk_category: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability
identified_risks.risk_description: Shared implementation responsibilities between SEG and PCU could create tensions or confusion about roles; weak capacity in MEHH; risk of insufficient ownership at government level
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project implementation manual to clarify roles and responsibilities; regular Steering Committee meetings; project management firm (AMO) to provide support and knowledge transfer; capacity building for PCU staff; performance reviews of PCU staff by MEHH and MEF

identified_risks.risk_description: High procurement risk due to MEHH's track record of delays and unfamiliarity with World Bank procedures; weak inputs from technical entities; delays in government approvals; poor contract execution
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Hands-on Expanded Implementation Support (HEIS) for procurement; recruitment of qualified procurement specialists (international expert and national specialist); development of procurement procedures manual; training on World Bank procedures; close World Bank supervision and oversight; prior and post review arrangements

identified_risks.risk_description: MEHH unfamiliar with World Bank financial management rules and procedures; PCU not yet operational; lack of staff and management tools; weak accounting framework implementation
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Recruitment of financial management specialist with World Bank experience; installation of Tompro accounting software; development and validation of financial procedures manual; recruitment of internal auditor; external audits by independent auditor; quarterly financial reporting; training on SYSCOHADA accounting standards

identified_risks.risk_description: High environmental risks related to Yessoulou IV water quality standards; air, water and soil pollution during civil works; high social risks from labor standards violations, resettlement, occupational health and safety, community health; limited implementation capacity and policy gaps
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Comprehensive Environmental and Social Impact Assessments (ESIA); Environmental and Social Commitment Plan (ESCP); Resettlement Action Plans (RAP); Labor Management Plan (LMP); Stakeholder Engagement Plan (SEP); process framework for Component 3; grievance redress mechanisms; close monitoring and compliance with ESF standards; environmental and social specialists in PCU

identified_risks.risk_description: Risk of prepaid meter rejection or vandalism; potential community resistance to tariff increases; stakeholder coordination challenges
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Establishment of Project Steering Committee with key stakeholders; awareness campaigns by SEG on tariff reforms and digital payment mechanisms; careful selection of pilot customers for prepaid meters (commercial clients first); community monitoring mechanisms; grievance redress mechanisms; regular stakeholder consultations

identified_risks.risk_description: Urban and coastal flooding risks (medium to high) in Conakry and Kindia regions; extreme heat and wildfire risks; water security concerns from changing rainfall patterns; climate-related hazards to infrastructure
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Climate and disaster risk screening assessment conducted; integration of climate resilience into detailed designs of water infrastructure; nature-based solutions to reduce erosion and sedimentation; solar energy for water production and distribution; reservoirs to mitigate flooding and drought; protection perimeters for boreholes; climate considerations in wastewater infrastructure design

identified_risks.risk_description: Project depends on timely implementation of parallel financing (Exim Bank India raw water intake, EIB dam safety upgrades, EIB-EU distribution network); delays in any component could affect overall project viability
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Joint implementation of EIB and IDA components under same Steering Committee, PCU and SEG unit; implementation support missions involving both EIB and World Bank task teams; staggered timeline for civil works implementation; all works scheduled for completion before project closing date; Government agreement to fund Exim Bank resettlement costs

identified_risks.risk_description: Long-term water security at risk due to climate change impacts, changing rainfall patterns, and land degradation in upstream areas; potential impact on water availability for Greater Conakry
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Nature-based solutions component to restore ecosystems and control erosion; Gangan National Park establishment and management; Basin Committee for integrated water resources management; water resources assessment and basin profile development; protection perimeters for boreholes; community engagement for sustainable water use; climate resilience integration into water sector planning

triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS 1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts
ESS 3: Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention and Management
ESS 5: Land Acquisition, Restrictions on Land Use and Involuntary Resettlement
ESS 6: Biodiversity Conservation and Sustainable Management of Living Natural Resources
ESS 10: Stakeholder Engagement and Information Disclosure
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: Yes - Project-level grievance mechanisms through PCU; World Bank Grievance Redress Service (GRS); World Bank Accountability Mechanism (Inspection Panel and Dispute Resolution Service); community monitoring mechanisms; annual satisfaction surveys; periodic stakeholder consultations
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Gender action plan developed; PDO indicator on average time women and girls spend collecting water (target 50% reduction); goal to increase female technical employees from 17% to 51% in water and sanitation sector; internship programs for young women; training workshops for women in technical roles; success stories highlighting women's achievements; awareness campaigns on gender equality benefits; WASH facilities in schools with menstrual hygiene considerations; women's involvement in technical decisions about standpipe construction; tracking of women in engineering positions and senior managerial roles at SEG; minimum 40% women among young people benefiting from professional opportunities
vulnerable_groups: Women and girls (disproportionate burden of water collection)
Local communities in Samou sub-basin and Gangan National Park area

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project addresses fragility through institutional strengthening and policy reforms that build state capacity and legitimacy; water sector reforms improve government service delivery and social contract; tariff reforms and utility turnaround support financial sustainability; sanitation governance reforms strengthen institutional framework; water resources management component promotes inclusive governance through Basin Committee; nature-based solutions provide livelihood opportunities for local communities; project design includes stakeholder engagement and grievance mechanisms to address community concerns
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Guinea is a fragile state with transitional government following September 2021 coup; political and governance risk rated HIGH; weak institutional capacity across water and sanitation sectors; limited financial resources for public services; poverty rate increased from 46.6% (2018) to 51.4% (2023); 63% of rural population lives in poverty; low human development index (182nd out of 192); gender inequality severe (182nd out of 191 countries); weak mining linkages to non-mining sectors; challenges in revenue mobilization; limited access to basic services (71% basic drinking water access, 31% basic sanitation); informal economy dominance in water and sanitation service provision
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: No active armed conflict in project area; however, political instability from transitional government creates uncertainty; wildfire risks in dry season around water infrastructure; security of water infrastructure from vandalism or sabotage (prepaid meter risk); need for OGPNRF rangers to conduct patrols in Gangan National Park to prevent deforestation and pollution; community security concerns related to resettlement and land acquisition; occupational health and safety risks during civil works implementation
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: Yes - Project includes involuntary resettlement component; Resettlement Action Plans (RAPs) prepared for Yessoulou IV water treatment plant and distribution network (Sub-Component 1.2); IDA financing US$13 million to cover compensation for project-affected persons (PAPs) including compensation for affected assets (land, houses, structures, crops/trees), assistance payments for relocation, loss of income, livelihood restoration programs, and assistance for vulnerable persons; resettlement costs cover IDA, EIB, EU and GoG financed activities; Exim Bank India resettlement costs to be funded by Government; RAPs to be prepared and adopted before disbursement; resettlement process managed by PCU with Commission Nationale de l'indemnisation; lessons learned from PUEG show importance of timely compensation to avoid delays in civil works
